text.title: AWS Multi-Account Landing Zone Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every application of `AWS Multi-Account Landing Zone` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: OU hierarchy MUST include at minimum: Security, Infrastructure, Workloads (Prod / Non-Prod / Sandbox sub-OUs), Suspended. Mixed prod / non-prod in a single OU is forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS Landing Zone Accelerator; CIS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Mixed OUs break SCP scoping; clear hierarchy makes guardrails crisp.

rule.statement: LZ MUST use AWS Control Tower OR a homegrown LZ Accelerator + AWS Config + GuardDuty + Security Hub + CloudTrail org-trail. Ad-hoc accounts without baseline are forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS Control Tower docs; AWS LZ Accelerator

rule.rationale: Manual baselines drift; Control Tower / LZA enforces baseline on every new account.

rule.statement: Org root SCP MUST deny: leave-org, disable-CloudTrail, disable-GuardDuty, modify-root, public-S3 (regulated OUs). Tested against canary OU first.

rationale.source: CIS AWS Benchmark; AWS SCP docs

rule.rationale: Without SCPs, compromised admin = compromised everything; SCPs are the org-level guardrail.

rule.statement: Human access MUST flow through IAM Identity Center permission sets, mapped to AD / Okta groups. Per-account IAM users forbidden for humans.
rule.rationale: Per-account IAM users = key sprawl + offboarding gap; SSO permission sets centralise.

rule.statement: Dedicated `log-archive` and `audit` accounts MUST receive CloudTrail org-trail logs, Config recordings, GuardDuty findings, Security Hub aggregation. Cross-account access tightly scoped.
rule.rationale: Co-locating logs with workloads makes log tampering trivial; isolated accounts are the auditor-friendly pattern.

rule.statement: New account creation MUST go through automated vending (Control Tower Account Factory, Service Catalog Custom AFT, or equivalent). Console-clicked accounts forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS Control Tower Account Factory; AFT docs

rule.rationale: Manual accounts skip baseline; automated vending applies guardrails + tags + network hooks consistently.

rule.statement: Multi-account networking MUST use Transit Gateway (or AWS Cloud WAN) hub-and-spoke from a Network account; flat per-account VPC peering forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS Transit Gateway docs; LZ Accelerator

rule.rationale: Full-mesh VPC peering scales O(n²) and breaks at ~20 accounts; TGW hub gives O(n).

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
